task_completion.description: Create the initial rectangular base box
task_completion.visual_evidence: A solid rectangular prism is visible in the FreeCAD 3D viewport, representing the 100x60x30mm outer dimensions.

task_completion.description: Hollow out the box to create an open top with uniform wall thickness
task_completion.visual_evidence: The top face of the box is open, revealing an interior cavity with visible walls and a floor (representing the 2mm thickness).

task_completion.description: Create four cylindrical internal standoffs on the floor
task_completion.visual_evidence: Four distinct cylindrical posts are visible on the inside floor of the box, arranged in a rectangular pattern.

task_completion.description: Create through-holes passing through the standoffs and the box floor
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visible holes in the center of each of the four cylindrical standoffs. If the bottom of the box is visible, the holes should pass completely through the floor.

task_completion.description: Create a USB port cutout on the right face
task_completion.visual_evidence: A square/rectangular hole (cutout) is visible on one of the shorter side walls (the right face) of the box.
The FreeCAD window title bar, tree view (combo view), or a save dialog shows the filename 'pcb_housing' or 'pcb_housing.FCStd'.

integrity.description: The agent used the FreeCAD GUI to design the model
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with FreeCAD's graphical interface (toolbars, combo view, 3D viewport) rather than just a terminal window.

integrity.description: The 3D model is genuinely rendered in the FreeCAD viewport
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D geometry is visible in the main FreeCAD 3D viewer area with standard FreeCAD shading, grid, or axis cross, not an imported image.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by executing a complete python script in the terminal
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows step-by-step modeling in the FreeCAD GUI or FreeCAD python console, rather than a single terminal command generating the entire file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/pcb_housing.FCStd. The final 3D model must have a bounding box of exactly 100mm (X) x 60mm (Y) x 30mm (Z) with a 1mm tolerance. The target volume of the solid is 29,612 mm³ (±5% tolerance). The model must feature a 2mm uniform wall/floor thickness, four 6mm-diameter standoffs with 2.5mm through-holes, and a 12x12mm USB cutout on the right face.
